column1: # Phase 2 mouse support: POINT-mode range extend on click.
# When a command has opened an auto-anchored range prompt,
# clicking a cell must drop the free end of the range on that
# cell (anchor stays). After ESC unanchors, click just moves the
# col D [32,41); body rows start at y=5.
# Seed a few cells so /RE has something to clear at commit time.
# /Range Erase — auto-anchored at A1, range reads A1..A1.
# Click C3 — range extends to A1..C3.
# Click B2 — range shrinks to A1..B2 (anchor unchanged).
# Enter commits: the A1..B2 range is erased, back to READY.
# Cells outside the committed range survive.
# Second /RE from a fresh pointer, then unanchor via ESC and
# verify that a click only moves the pointer.
# After first ESC the range collapses but POINT persists.
# Click D4 — pointer jumps; anchor stays cleared so the range
# stays collapsed at the new pointer.
# Second ESC cancels — the 99 we seeded at A1 is untouched.
